chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّهُ يُعَقُّ يَوْمَ السَّابِعِ لِلْمَوْلُودِ وَ يُحْلَقُ رَأْسُهُ وَ يُسَمَّى
chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – Aqeeqa is one the seventh day for the new-born, and shaving of its head and naming

text: الصَّبِيُّ إِذَا وُلِدَ عُقَّ عَنْهُ وَ حُلِقَ رَأْسُهُ يُتَصَدَّقُ بِوَزْنِ الشَّعْرِ وَ أُهْدِيَ إِلَى الْقَابِلَةِ الرِّجْلُ مَعَ الْوَرِكِ وَ يُدْعَى نَفَرٌ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَيَأْكُلُونَ وَ يَدْعُونَ لِلْغُلَامِ وَ يُسَمَّى يَوْمَ السَّابِعِ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moala Bin Muhammad, from Al Washa, from Aban, from Hafs Al Kunasy,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘The child, when it is born, perform Aqeeqa for him, and shave off his head, give in charity by the weight of the hair, and gift to the mid-wife, the leg (of the sacrificed animal) along with the leaf (silver or gold), and invite a number from the Muslims, so they should be earring and supplicating for the boy, and name him on the seventh day’.
en.sarwar: 12. Al-Husayn from Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla’ ibn Muhammad from al-Washsha’ from Aban from Hafs al-Kunasiy who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has stated this Hadith. ‘When ‘Aqiqah is offered for a child, his head is shaved, equal to the weight of his hair charity is given, one leg and hip is given to al-Qabilah (the special nurse, doctor), Muslims should be invited and when they are served they should pray for the boy. A name can be given to the child on the seventh day of his birth.’”
